pattern: Sort the array; use a DP where dp[v] = length of the longest consecutive sequence ending at integer value v, updating in sorted order with either v or v+1 as the chosen element.
hints: Each element can be kept as-is or incremented by 1; you want to select a subset that forms a run of consecutive integers after this optional increment.
Sort the array and process elements in order; for each element x consider placing it as value x (kept) or x+1 (incremented) in the consecutive sequence.

Maintain dp[v] = length of the longest sequence ending at value v; for element x, dp[x+1] = max(dp[x+1], dp[x]+1) and dp[x] = max(dp[x], dp[x-1]+1), taking care to process kept-as-x before incremented-to-x+1 within the same original value.

edge_inputs.description: example with three consecutive after increments
no two elements can be made consecutive
all same elements, at most two usable (x and x+1)
gap of two cannot be bridged by a single increment
choosing to increment one blocks another from being used
large cluster that becomes 3 consecutive with smart increments

stress.description: random array with values clustered near each other
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, max(2, n // 5)) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
